# DevToolkit — Full Site Content for AI

> Free, browser-based developer tools at oortcraft.dev. No sign-up, no data sent to servers, 100% private.

## Site Overview

DevToolkit provides fast, private, open-source developer utilities that run entirely in the browser. All processing happens client-side using JavaScript and the Web Crypto API. No data is ever sent to a server. The site is free to use with no registration, no paywall, and no usage limits.

**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev
**Technology**: Built with Astro, React, and Tailwind CSS. Deployed on Vercel.

---

## Tools

### 1. JSON Formatter & Beautifier
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/json-formatter
**Description**: Format, beautify, and minify JSON data with customizable indentation (2 spaces, 4 spaces, or tabs).
**How to use**: Paste raw or minified JSON into the input area. Click "Format" to beautify or "Minify" to compress. Supports inputs up to 1MB.
**Use cases**: Debugging API responses, reading configuration files, preparing JSON for documentation, minifying JSON for production.

### 2. JSON Validator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/json-validator
**Description**: Validate JSON syntax and get detailed error messages with line numbers.
**How to use**: Paste JSON data and click "Validate". Shows whether JSON is syntactically correct per RFC 8259. Reports errors with exact location. Shows stats (top-level keys, max depth, array items) for valid JSON.
**Common errors detected**: Trailing commas, single quotes, unescaped characters, missing brackets, undefined/NaN values.

### 3. JSON to YAML Converter
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/json-to-yaml
**Description**: Convert JSON data to YAML format instantly as you type.
**How to use**: Paste JSON into the left editor. YAML output appears automatically. Use "Copy" to copy the result. Supports inputs up to 1MB.
**Key facts**: YAML is a superset of JSON, so conversion is always lossless. YAML supports comments (#) while JSON does not. YAML is preferred for configuration files (Docker Compose, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions).

### 4. Base64 Encoder / Decoder
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/base64
**Description**: Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 back to plain text.
**How to use**: Select Encode or Decode mode. Conversion happens automatically as you type.
**Key facts**: Base64 is an encoding (not encryption). It uses 64 ASCII characters (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, +, /). Output is ~33% larger than input. Commonly used for embedding images in HTML/CSS data URIs, encoding binary data for JSON APIs, and email attachments (MIME).

### 5. URL Encoder / Decoder
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/url-encoder
**Description**: Encode or decode URL components for safe transmission using percent-encoding.
**How to use**: Select Encode or Decode mode. Conversion happens automatically as you type.
**Key facts**: Uses encodeURIComponent (encodes all special characters including /, ?, =). Spaces become %20. Used for encoding query string parameters, form data, and special characters in API endpoints.

### 6. UUID Generator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/uuid-generator
**Description**: Generate random UUIDs (v4) or time-based UUIDs (v1).
**How to use**: Select UUID version, click Generate. Copy button available. Keeps history of last 5 generated UUIDs.
**UUID versions**: v4 (random, 122 bits of randomness, most commonly used), v1 (time-based, includes timestamp, useful for sortable IDs).
**Use cases**: Database primary keys, session tokens, API keys, idempotency keys, unique file names, distributed systems.

### 7. Hash Generator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/hash-generator
**Description**: Generate MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512 hashes from text using Web Crypto API.
**How to use**: Select algorithm, type or paste text. Hash computed automatically as you type.
**Algorithms**: MD5 (fast, cryptographically broken, checksums only), SHA-1 (deprecated for security), SHA-256 (recommended, used in TLS and Bitcoin), SHA-512 (longer hash, used in some password hashing).
**Key facts**: Hash functions are one-way and cannot be reversed. Same input always produces same hash.

### 8. Lorem Ipsum Generator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/lorem-ipsum
**Description**: Generate 1-10 paragraphs of lorem ipsum placeholder text.
**How to use**: Select number of paragraphs (1-10), click Generate. Starts with classic "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet..." followed by randomized Latin-style text.
**Use cases**: Filling mockups and wireframes, testing typography and layout, populating templates, demonstrating font styles.

### 9. JWT Decoder & Validator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/jwt-decoder
**Description**: Decode, inspect, and validate JSON Web Tokens instantly in the browser.
**How to use**: Paste a JWT token. The tool automatically decodes and displays the header (algorithm, type), payload (claims, data), and signature information. Shows issued/expiration timestamps.
**Key facts**: JWTs consist of three Base64URL-encoded parts separated by dots. Decoding happens entirely client-side. Supports all JWT algorithms (HS256, RS256, ES256, etc.).

### 10. Regex Tester
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/regex-tester
**Description**: Test regular expressions with real-time matching and highlighting.
**How to use**: Enter a regex pattern and test string. Matches appear in real-time with index positions and matched text. Toggle flags (g, i, m, s) with buttons. Capture groups are displayed for each match.
**Use cases**: Validating regex patterns, debugging complex expressions, learning regex syntax, testing patterns before using in code.

### 11. Diff Checker
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/diff-checker
**Description**: Compare two texts and see differences highlighted line by line.
**How to use**: Paste original text on the left and modified text on the right. Differences appear below with green highlights for additions and red highlights for deletions. Shows stats (additions, deletions, unchanged lines).
**Key facts**: Uses LCS (Longest Common Subsequence) algorithm. Line-based comparison. Runs entirely in the browser.

### 12. Color Converter
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/color-converter
**Description**: Convert colors between HEX, RGB, and HSL formats instantly.
**How to use**: Enter any color value (HEX, RGB, or HSL format). All three format outputs update in real-time with a color preview swatch. Copy any format with one click.
**Supported formats**: HEX (3/6/8 digit), RGB (rgb(r, g, b)), HSL (hsl(h, s%, l%)), CSS named colors.

### 13. Timestamp Converter
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/timestamp-converter
**Description**: Convert Unix timestamps to human-readable dates and vice versa.
**How to use**: Enter a Unix timestamp (seconds or milliseconds) to see ISO 8601, UTC, local time, and relative time. Use the reverse section to convert a date string back to a Unix timestamp. Click "Now" to use the current time.
**Key facts**: Unix time counts seconds since January 1, 1970 (epoch). Supports both seconds and milliseconds. All conversion happens client-side.

### 14. Cron Expression Builder
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/cron-builder
**Description**: Build, test, and understand cron expressions with a visual editor.
**How to use**: Enter a cron expression or use preset buttons (Every minute, Every 5 min, Every hour, etc.). See a human-readable description and the next 5 scheduled run times. Supports standard 5-field cron syntax and special strings (@hourly, @daily, @weekly, @monthly, @yearly).
**Cron fields**: minute (0-59), hour (0-23), day of month (1-31), month (1-12), day of week (0-6).

### 15. Markdown Preview
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/markdown-preview
**Description**: Write Markdown and see real-time rendered preview side by side.
**How to use**: Type or paste Markdown in the left editor. The right panel shows the rendered HTML preview in real-time. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown including tables, task lists, strikethrough, and fenced code blocks.
**Key facts**: Uses the marked library for parsing and DOMPurify for XSS prevention. All processing happens client-side.

### 16. CSS Minifier
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/css-minifier
**Description**: Minify CSS code to reduce file size and improve page load times.
**How to use**: Paste CSS code in the left panel. Minified output appears automatically in the right panel. Shows stats (original size, minified size, savings percentage).
**Key facts**: Removes comments, unnecessary whitespace, trailing semicolons, and redundant characters. Does not change selectors, properties, or values. Typical savings range from 15-40%.

### 17. JSON Schema Validator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/json-schema-validator
**Description**: Validate JSON data against JSON Schema with detailed error reporting.
**How to use**: Enter a JSON Schema in the left editor and JSON data in the right editor. Click "Validate" to check conformance. Use "Load Sample" to see an example. Shows detailed error messages with paths and validation keywords.
**Key facts**: Uses Ajv (Another JSON Schema Validator) supporting Draft-07. Local $ref references supported. All validation happens client-side.

### 18. HTML Entity Encoder & Decoder
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/html-entity-encoder
**Description**: Encode and decode HTML entities for safe web content.
**How to use**: Select Encode or Decode mode. Type or paste text and the output updates in real-time. Handles named entities (&amp;, &lt;, &gt;, &quot;, &apos;) and numeric entities (&#123;, &#x7B;).
**Key facts**: Encoding converts special characters to HTML entities to prevent browsers from interpreting them as markup. Essential for displaying code snippets in HTML and preventing XSS.

### 19. Password Generator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/password-generator
**Description**: Generate strong, random passwords with customizable length and character options.
**How to use**: Adjust the length slider (1-128), toggle character types (uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols), and click Generate. Copy the result with one click.
**Key facts**: Uses crypto.getRandomValues() for cryptographically secure randomness. All generation happens client-side — no passwords are ever sent to a server. Includes a strength indicator (Weak/Medium/Strong).

### 20. String Case Converter
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/case-converter
**Description**: Convert text between camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, kebab-case, Title Case, UPPERCASE, and lowercase.
**How to use**: Type or paste text in the input area, select the target case format from the pill selector, and the output updates in real-time. Copy the result with one click.
**Key facts**: Automatically detects word boundaries from camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, kebab-case, and space-separated text. Supports 8 case formats commonly used in programming.

### 21. Base Converter
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/base-converter
**Description**: Convert numbers between binary, octal, decimal, and hexadecimal bases.
**How to use**: Select the input base (BIN, OCT, DEC, HEX), enter a number, and see all four base conversions in real-time. Prefixes like 0x, 0b, 0o are automatically stripped.
**Key facts**: Uses BigInt for arbitrary precision — handles numbers of any size. Validates input characters for the selected base. All conversion happens client-side.

### 22. YAML Validator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/yaml-validator
**Description**: Validate YAML syntax and convert between YAML and JSON formats.
**How to use**: Paste YAML in the input area. Choose Validate mode for syntax checking with stats (keys, depth, documents), or YAML → JSON mode for conversion. Results update in real-time.
**Key facts**: Supports multi-document YAML (--- separator). Shows line/column error info for invalid YAML. Uses js-yaml library for parsing.

### 23. SQL Formatter
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/sql-formatter
**Description**: Format and beautify SQL queries with dialect-specific formatting.
**How to use**: Paste SQL in the input, select a dialect (Standard, PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, T-SQL, PL/SQL), then click Format or Minify.
**Key facts**: Supports 6 SQL dialects. Uses the sql-formatter library. Uppercase keywords, proper indentation. Minify mode collapses whitespace.

### 24. CSV to JSON Converter
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/csv-to-json
**Description**: Convert CSV data to JSON format with automatic delimiter detection.
**How to use**: Paste CSV data. Toggle Header Row (uses first row as keys) and Type Inference (converts numbers/booleans). Conversion is real-time.
**Key facts**: Auto-detects comma, semicolon, tab, and pipe delimiters. Uses PapaParse library. Shows row count. Handles quoted fields and escaped characters.

### 25. QR Code Generator
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/qr-code-generator
**Description**: Generate QR codes from text, URLs, or any data. Download as SVG or PNG.
**How to use**: Enter text or URL, select error correction level (L/M/Q/H), and the QR code generates in real-time. Download as SVG (vector) or PNG (512×512).
**Key facts**: Supports up to 4,296 characters. Four error correction levels. All generation is client-side using the qrcode library.

### 26. JSON Path Finder
**URL**: https://oortcraft.dev/tools/jsonpath-finder
**Description**: Query JSON data using JSONPath expressions with real-time results.
**How to use**: Paste JSON in the left panel, enter a JSONPath expression (e.g. $.store.books[*].title). Results update in real-time with match count. Quick-insert buttons for common expressions.
**Key facts**: Uses the Goessner JSONPath specification via jsonpath-plus. Supports filters, recursive descent, array slicing. Shows match count.

---

## Blog

The DevToolkit blog publishes developer tutorials and guides related to the tools offered on the site.

### Published Articles

1. **What is JSON? A Complete Guide for Developers**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/what-is-json
   Topics: JSON syntax, data types, common use cases, JSON vs XML comparison.

2. **What is UUID? A Developer's Guide**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/what-is-uuid
   Topics: UUID format, versions (v1-v5), when to use UUIDs, collision probability.

3. **URL Encoding Explained**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/url-encoding-explained
   Topics: Percent-encoding, reserved characters, encodeURI vs encodeURIComponent, common pitfalls.

4. **What is Base64?**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/what-is-base64
   Topics: Base64 encoding algorithm, use cases, Base64 vs encryption, data URI scheme.

5. **JSON vs XML**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/json-vs-xml
   Topics: Format comparison, when to use each, migration strategies, modern best practices.

6. **What is JWT? JSON Web Tokens Explained**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/what-is-jwt
   Topics: JWT structure, header/payload/signature, authentication flow, common claims, security best practices.

7. **Regex Cheat Sheet for Developers**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/regex-cheat-sheet
   Topics: Regex syntax reference, character classes, quantifiers, groups, lookahead/lookbehind, common patterns.

8. **Text Diff Algorithms Explained**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/text-diff-explained
   Topics: LCS algorithm, Myers diff, how Git uses diff, unified diff format, practical applications.

9. **Color Formats Explained: HEX, RGB, and HSL**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/color-formats-guide
   Topics: Color representation, HEX/RGB/HSL formats, when to use each, accessibility, CSS color functions.

10. **Unix Timestamps Explained: Epoch Time for Developers**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/unix-timestamp-guide
    Topics: Unix time, epoch, seconds vs milliseconds, Y2038 problem, JavaScript/Python examples.

11. **Cron Expressions Explained: A Complete Guide**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/cron-expression-guide
    Topics: Cron syntax, five-field format, special characters, common patterns, crontab, timezone considerations.

12. **Markdown Syntax Guide: From Basics to Advanced**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/markdown-syntax-guide
    Topics: Markdown syntax, headings, lists, links, images, code blocks, tables, GFM features, task lists.

13. **CSS Minification Explained: Why and How to Minify CSS**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/css-minification-explained
    Topics: CSS minification, web performance, Core Web Vitals, build tools, PostCSS, cssnano, source maps.

14. **JSON Schema Tutorial: Validate Your Data Like a Pro**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/json-schema-tutorial
    Topics: JSON Schema, data validation, types, properties, required fields, arrays, combining schemas, real-world examples.

15. **HTML Entities: A Complete Guide to Character Encoding**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/html-entities-guide
    Topics: HTML entities, character encoding, named entities, numeric entities, UTF-8, XSS prevention, special characters.

16. **How to Create Strong Passwords: A Developer's Guide**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/strong-password-guide
    Topics: Password strength, common mistakes, brute-force attacks, entropy, password managers, best practices for 2026.

17. **Naming Conventions in Programming: camelCase vs snake_case vs kebab-case**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/naming-conventions-guide
    Topics: Naming conventions, camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, kebab-case, SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, language-specific conventions.

18. **Base64 Encoding in JavaScript, Python & Go: Practical Examples**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/base64-encoding-examples
    Topics: Base64 encoding/decoding, JavaScript (browser & Node.js), Python, Go, copy-paste-ready code examples.

19. **SHA-256 vs SHA-512: Which Hash Algorithm Should You Use?**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/sha256-vs-sha512
    Topics: SHA-256, SHA-512, hash comparison, performance benchmarks, security considerations, real-world use cases.

20. **YAML Tutorial for Beginners: Syntax, Examples & Best Practices**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/yaml-tutorial
    Topics: YAML syntax, scalars, sequences, mappings, multi-line strings, anchors, Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions.

21. **UUID v1 vs v4 vs v7: Which UUID Version Should You Use?**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/uuid-versions-compared
    Topics: UUID versions, v1 (time-based), v4 (random), v7 (sortable), database performance, distributed systems.

22. **What is Lorem Ipsum? History, Purpose & Modern Usage**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/what-is-lorem-ipsum
    Topics: Lorem Ipsum history, Cicero, placeholder text, web design, typography, modern alternatives.

23. **What is Hashing? MD5, SHA-256, SHA-512 Explained**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/what-is-hashing
    Topics: Cryptographic hash functions, MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512, one-way functions, collision resistance, code examples.

24. **Number Base Systems Explained: Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/number-base-systems-guide
    Topics: Positional notation, binary, octal, hexadecimal, base conversion, Unix permissions, color codes, memory addresses.

25. **YAML Validation Best Practices: Avoiding Common Pitfalls**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/yaml-validation-best-practices
    Topics: YAML syntax, indentation, Norway problem, multiline strings, YAML vs JSON, multi-document YAML, validation tools.

26. **SQL Formatting Guide: Writing Clean, Readable Queries**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/sql-formatting-guide
    Topics: SQL formatting, keyword casing, indentation, dialect differences, PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, T-SQL, anti-patterns.

27. **CSV to JSON Conversion: Methods, Libraries, and Best Practices**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/csv-to-json-conversion
    Topics: CSV parsing, JSON conversion, PapaParse, type inference, delimiter handling, header rows, edge cases.

28. **QR Codes for Developers: Generation, Error Correction, and Best Practices**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/qr-code-guide
    Topics: QR code anatomy, error correction levels, encoding modes, data capacity, SVG vs PNG, programmatic generation.

29. **JSONPath Tutorial: Querying JSON Data Like a Pro**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/jsonpath-tutorial
    Topics: JSONPath syntax, dot/bracket notation, wildcards, recursive descent, filter expressions, jsonpath-plus, JSONPath vs jq.

30. **Common Regex Patterns: 20+ Examples for Everyday Development**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/common-regex-patterns
    Topics: Email, URL, IP, phone validation patterns, data extraction, text processing, security patterns, JavaScript and Python regex examples.

31. **Password Entropy Explained: How Strong Is Your Password**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/password-entropy-explained
    Topics: Shannon entropy, entropy formula, brute-force crack times, length vs complexity, dictionary attacks, rainbow tables, CSPRNG generation.

32. **Crontab Cheat Sheet: 30+ Cron Job Examples**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/crontab-cheat-sheet
    Topics: Cron syntax, special characters, common schedules, backup/monitoring/deployment examples, special strings, crontab management commands.

33. **bcrypt vs scrypt vs Argon2: Password Hashing Compared**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/bcrypt-vs-scrypt-vs-argon2
    Topics: Password hashing algorithms, bcrypt cost factors, scrypt memory-hardness, Argon2id, GPU attack resistance, OWASP recommendations, migration strategies, benchmark data.

34. **How to Debug JWT Authentication Issues: A Practical Decoder Guide**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/jwt-debugging-guide
    Topics: JWT decoding, jwt decode, debugging JWT errors, TokenExpiredError, invalid signature, algorithm mismatch, aud/iss validation, base64url decoding, security best practices.

35. **JSON to YAML Conversion: A Practical Guide for Kubernetes, Docker, and Helm**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/json-to-yaml-devops
    Topics: JSON to YAML, yq converter, Kubernetes manifests, Helm values.yaml, Docker Compose, Python yaml.dump, js-yaml, multiline strings, boolean hazards, DevOps config formats.

36. **Measuring CSS Performance: LCP, FCP, and Core Web Vitals Workflow for Developers**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/css-performance-guide
    Topics: CSS performance optimization, render-blocking CSS, critical CSS inlining, LCP, FCP, Core Web Vitals, PostCSS cssnano, PageSpeed Insights, build tool integration.

37. **The Algorithm Behind git diff: How Myers Diff Works**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/myers-diff-algorithm
    Topics: Myers diff algorithm, git diff internals, edit graph, shortest edit script, D-paths, k-diagonal traversal, unified diff format, Patience vs Histogram vs Myers comparison.

38. **Regex for Email Validation: Patterns That Actually Work**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/regex-email-validation
    Topics: Email regex patterns, simple to RFC-aware progressive validation, pass/fail test matrices, common regex mistakes, JavaScript Python Go examples, RFC 5322, plus-addressing, TLD length.

39. **Epoch to Date: Convert Unix Timestamps in JS, Python, Go, Java, PHP & Rust**
    URL: https://oortcraft.dev/blog/epoch-to-date-conversion
    Topics: Epoch to date conversion, Unix timestamp to datetime, JavaScript Python Go Java PHP Rust code snippets, timezone-aware conversion, milliseconds vs seconds, DST pitfalls, Bash CLI.

---

## Tool Categories

Browse tools by category:

1. **JSON Tools** — https://oortcraft.dev/tools/category/json
   Format, validate, convert, and inspect JSON data. Includes JSON Formatter, JSON Validator, JSON to YAML Converter, JSON Schema Validator, YAML Validator, CSV to JSON Converter, and JSON Path Finder.

2. **Encoding Tools** — https://oortcraft.dev/tools/category/encoding
   Encode and decode Base64, URLs, HTML entities, and number bases. Includes Base Converter for binary/octal/decimal/hex.

3. **Generator Tools** — https://oortcraft.dev/tools/category/generator
   Generate UUIDs (v4/v1), cryptographically secure passwords, Lorem Ipsum placeholder text, and QR codes.

4. **Security Tools** — https://oortcraft.dev/tools/category/security
   Compute MD5/SHA-1/SHA-256/SHA-512 hashes and decode JWT tokens. Uses Web Crypto API.

5. **Text Tools** — https://oortcraft.dev/tools/category/text
   Test regex patterns, compare text diffs, preview Markdown, convert between 8 string case formats, and format SQL queries.

6. **CSS Tools** — https://oortcraft.dev/tools/category/css
   Convert colors between HEX/RGB/HSL and minify CSS for production.

7. **DevOps Tools** — https://oortcraft.dev/tools/category/devops
   Build, test, and understand cron expressions with a visual editor.

8. **Date & Time Tools** — https://oortcraft.dev/tools/category/date
   Convert Unix timestamps to human-readable dates and vice versa.

---

## Comparisons

In-depth comparisons between related tools and technologies:

1. **SHA-256 vs SHA-512: Which Hash Algorithm Should You Use?**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/compare/sha256-vs-sha512
   Topics: Output size (256 vs 512 bits), performance on 32-bit vs 64-bit CPUs, collision resistance, real-world use cases (TLS, Bitcoin, Ed25519, DNSSEC).

2. **Base64 vs URL Encoding: What's the Difference?**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/compare/base64-vs-url-encoding
   Topics: Binary transport vs URL safety, size overhead (33% vs variable), character sets, double-encoding pitfalls, Base64URL variant.

3. **JSON vs YAML: Which Data Format Should You Use?**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/compare/json-vs-yaml
   Topics: Syntax comparison, comments support, multi-line strings, YAML superset relationship, Norway problem, API vs configuration use cases.

4. **JSON Formatter vs JSON Validator: What's the Difference?**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/compare/json-formatter-vs-validator
   Topics: Formatting vs validation, when to use each, combined workflow, common JSON syntax errors, schema validation.

5. **CSS Minifier vs CSS Beautifier: When to Compress, When to Expand**
   URL: https://oortcraft.dev/compare/css-minifier-vs-beautifier
   Topics: Minification savings (15-40%), Core Web Vitals impact, build tool integration, development workflow.

---

## Key Features

- **Lightning Fast**: All tools run in the browser with no server round-trips.
- **100% Private**: Data never leaves the device. No tracking, no storage.
- **Free Forever**: No sign-up, no paywall, no limits.
- **Open Source**: Built with modern web technologies (Astro, React, Tailwind CSS).

---

## AI Policy

This site welcomes AI crawlers and indexing. Content on oortcraft.dev may be freely used for AI training, referenced in AI-generated responses, and cited in search results. Please attribute to "DevToolkit (oortcraft.dev)" when citing.

Contact: https://oortcraft.dev
